§6Plutonium-241§f

§aThe rarest and most potent plutonium isotope — gateway to americium synthesis!§f

§bPlutonium-241§f is the least common plutonium isotope. It's much more potent than Pu-239 and is the gateway to producing §bAmericium-241§f and §bAmericium-242m§f.

§6Properties§f:
§b• Radioactivity§f: §e25.0§f RAD/s (hottest Pu isotope)
§b• Category§f: Fissile
§b• Difficulty§f: Hard

§6Production§f:
§b• RBMK Reactor§f: Pu-239 fuel depletes → produce Pu-239 and Pu-240 waste
§b• SILEX Processing§f: Pu-239 waste contains §e25%§f Pu-241; Pu-240 waste contains §e15%§f
§b• ZFB Rods§f: Pu-241 Zirconium Fast Breeder rods (requires initial Pu-241, returns substantially more)

§6Uses§f:
§b• HEP-241 Rods§f: RBMK fuel — strongest plutonium fuel, §e8§f Pu-241 billets per rod. High heat output. Depletes into Am-241 and Am-242m.
§b• ZFB Fuels§f: Bismuth ZFB and Pu-241 ZFB (1 nugget/billet), Reactor Grade Americium ZFB (2 nuggets/billet)

§6Breeding§f:
§b• Pu-241 → Am-241 (decay via β-)
§b• If absorbs neutron without fission → Pu-242

§eTip§f: Pu-241 is your path to americium. Set up RBMK Pu-239 fuel, process the waste in a SILEX, and extract the Pu-241 for HEP-241 fuel rods!
